persons.arguments_against: Single-initial warning applies, and there is a second Woodall biography in the corpus, so the counter-hypothesis is that the other Woodall (or an unrelated J. Woodall) held the Bermuda post. This fails: the stint office is Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Bermuda, a unique single-occupant post, and the biography records 'gov. and c.-in-c., Berm., 1955-59', enclosing the stint's 1956-1958 listing exactly. The staff list prints 'Lieut.-Gen. Sir John Woodall' in 1958, matching the bio's 'Lieut.-Gen. Sir John' precisely in rank, knighthood, and full forename, and three honours agree (K.B.E., C.B., M.C. in 1956; C.B. thereafter). The other Woodall in the staff lists would also have to be a knighted Lieut.-Gen. named John with the same three honours governing Bermuda over the same years — not a realistic alternative.
persons.would_overturn: External evidence that a different John Woodall governed Bermuda 1956-1958, or that the bio's governorship clause is a conflation — neither plausible given rank, honours, and tenure all agree.

notes: position_sim FAIL is the recurring abbreviation artifact ('gov. and c.-in-c.' = 'Governor and Commander-in-Chief'). Multiple independent dimensions: unique office + exact tenure containment (1955-59 vs 1956-58), full forename and rank agreement, and a three-honour overlap.
